purpose: Incremental GS LinkOps AI research ingestion candidates

records.name: IN-SPACe / NRSC Shadnagar private GSaaS infrastructure model
records.summary: IN-SPACe reportedly invited up to three Indian companies to establish and operate ground stations inside the NRSC Shadnagar campus under a GSaaS model. Publicly reported parameters include S/X/Ka-band support, approximately 9.15 acres, Ka-band interference protection, 10- or 20-year infrastructure leases, and a plug-and-play option for antennas up to 7.5 m designed for 200 km/h wind conditions.

extractable_fields.operator_model: private GSaaS operator on government space infrastructure

database_targets: Site Environment and RF Risk Table
records.confidence_reason: Detailed contemporary reporting, but the underlying official EOI document was not located in this scan.
pending_confirmation: official EOI URL and document number

records.name: Optical Ground Stations for Space Communications: Systems Engineering, Availability, and Service Economics Through 2030
records.summary: The paper argues that optical ground-station bottlenecks are shifting from peak line rate to repeatable service availability under weather, acquisition, scheduling and operational constraints. It proposes a hybrid optical-plus-RF baseline, with optical used for throughput and RF used for continuity, contingency and assured command paths.
records.confidence_reason: Recent technical preprint with explicit systems and economics framing; values require validation against primary mission and vendor sources.

records.name: SkyGS multi-provider GSaaS and cloud scheduling framework
records.summary: SkyGS models joint scheduling across multiple GSaaS and cloud providers rather than binding missions to one provider. It combines contact selection and downstream compute placement, using long-term latency constraints and real-time optimization. Reported simulation results show potential cost savings up to 63% and average latency reduction up to 95% under the tested assumptions.
records.confidence_reason: Clear optimization model and reproducible research framing, but operational gains are simulation-dependent.
